title: Corynotheca divaricata
taxonomy: Corynotheca divaricata is classified within the family Asphodelaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the asphodels or lilioid monocots. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Corynotheca divaricata being part of the subfamily Asphodeloideae. The Asphodelaceae family belongs to the order Asparagales, which is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous flowering plants. Asparagales is a part of the monocot clade, a group of flowering plants that also includes the order Alismatales, among others. Further classification of Corynotheca divaricata is at the genus and species level, with Corynotheca being a monotypic genus.

intro: Corynotheca divaricata, also known as the Divaricate Corynotheca, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the family Asphodelaceae. The Divaricate Corynotheca is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a promising remedy for treating wounds, infections, and certain skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of the plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
